{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_f8efaee5300c46bd80bdf46f","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tSSE broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n3\t\n4\tManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n5\t\"\"\"\n6\t\n7\timport asyncio\n8\timport json\n9\timport logging\n10\timport os\n11\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\n12\tfrom datetime import datetime\n13\tfrom typing import Dict\n14\tfrom uuid import uuid4\n15\t\n16\t# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n17\t# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n18\t# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n19\t# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n20\t# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n21\t# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n22\t#\n23\t#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n24\t#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n25\t#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n26\t#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n27\t#\n28\t# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n29\t# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n30\t# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n31\t# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n32\t# reconnection tests.\n33\tKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n34\t    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n35\t)\n36\t\n37\t# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n38\t# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n39\t# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n40\t# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n41\t# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n42\t# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n43\t# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n44\t# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n45\t# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n46\t_DROP = object()\n47\t\n48\t\n49\tlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n50\t\n51\t\n52\t@dataclass\n53\tclass broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n\nManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\n# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n#\n#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n#\n# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n# reconnection tests.\nKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n)\n\n# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n_DROP = object()\n\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEConnection:\n    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n    connection_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEEvent:\n    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\"\"\"\n    event_type: str\n    data: dict\n    target_session_id: str | None = None\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n\n\nclass SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n\n    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n        self._running = False\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._cleanup_task:\n            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._cleanup_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n    def register(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> SSEConnection:\n        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n        connection = SSEConnection(\n            connection_id=connection_id,\n            surface_id=surface_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n        return connection\n\n    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n            del self.connections[connection_id]\n\n    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n\n        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        for conn in self.connections.values():\n            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n                count += 1\n        if count:\n            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n        return count\n\n    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n\n        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n        \"\"\"\n        sent_count = 0\n\n        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n            # Filter by target\n            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n                continue\n            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n\n            # Queue the event\n            try:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n                sent_count += 1\n            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n\n        return sent_count\n\n    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n\n        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n            })\n\n            while True:\n                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n                try:\n                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n                        connection.queue.get(),\n                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n                    )\n                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                    yield \": ping\\n\\n\"\n                    continue\n\n                # Abrupt drop sentinel (see _DROP): end the stream WITHOUT a\n                # disconnect event so the browser EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n                if event is _DROP:\n                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    return\n\n                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n\n                # Format and yield the event\n                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, event.data)\n\n                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    break\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n\n    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n\n                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n                dead_connections = [\n                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n                ]\n\n                for cid in dead_connections:\n                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n                    try:\n                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n                        ))\n                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                        pass\n                    self.unregister(cid)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n\n\n# Global broadcaster instance\n_broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster | None = None\n\n\ndef get_broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n    global _broadcaster\n    if _broadcaster is None:\n        _broadcaster = SSEBroadcaster()\n    return _broadcaster\n\n\n# Event types\nclass EventType:\n    \"\"\"SSE event types.\"\"\"\n\n    # Connection lifecycle\n    CONNECTED = \"connected\"\n    DISCONNECT = \"disconnect\"\n    HEARTBEAT = \"heartbeat\"\n\n    # Result events\n    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n\n    # Component events (Phase 2)\n    COMPONENT_UPDATED = \"component_updated\"\n\n    # Intent events\n    INTENT_PENDING = \"intent_pending\"\n    INTENT_DISPATCHED = \"intent_dispatched\"\n    INTENT_RESOLVED = \"intent_resolved\"\n\n    # That proves the *server* still delivers\n * to a reconnected stream — but it never drives the browser's own EventSource\n * state machine (onopen → loadTopics, onerror, addEventListener('result_created')\n * → loadTopics, the 'disconnect' → eventSource.close()). That second onopen\n * is the canvas's reconnect path, and it calls loadTopics() (the re-render\n * the AC names). We model reconnect by firing onopen again on the live\n * EventSource — faithful to real browser behavior.\n *\n * The Python driver (tests/test_canvas_eventsource_reconnect.py) feeds a JSON\n * test --- minimal DOM shim --------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// --- setup mock document FIRST (before loading canvas.js) --------------------\n\nconst elementsById = {};\n\nclass El {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._classHistory = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = [];\n        this._style = {};\n        this._disabled = false;\n        this._value = \"\";\n        this._title = \"\";\n        this._textHistory = [];\n        // For style.display, wrap in a proxy to track changes\n        this._display = \"\";\n    }\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n        this._classHistory.push(this._classes.join(\" \"));\n    }\n    get className() { return this._classes.join(\" \"); }\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) { if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c); },\n            remove(c) { self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c); },\n            contains(c) { return self._classes.includes(c); },\n        };\n    }\n    get dataset() { return this._dataset; }\n    get style() {\n        // Return a proxy that tracks style.display changes\n        return new Proxy(this._style, {\n            set: (target, prop, value) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    this._display = value;\n                }\n                target[prop] = value;\n                return true;\n            },\n            get: (target, prop) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    return this._display;\n                }\n                return target[prop];\n            }\n        });\n    }\n    get disabled() { return this._disabled; }\n    set disabled(v) { this._disabled = !!v; }\n    get value() { return this._value; }\n    set value(v) { this._value = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get title() { return this._title; }\n    set title(v) { this._title = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get scrollHeight() { return 40; }\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        this._textHistory.push(this._textContent);\n    }\n    get textContent() { return this._textContent; }\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        if (this._innerHTML === \"\") this._children = [];   // container.innerHTML = ''\n    }\n    get innerHTML() {\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => {\n                // Render text nodes (tag === \"#text\") as escaped text\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._innerHTML || escapeText(c._textContent || \"\");\n                }\n                // Render element nodes via outerHTML\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n    appendChild(c) {\n        // Handle DocumentFragment: spill its children into this element\n        // Fragments have _children array but no _tag property (elements always have _tag)\n        if (c && c._children && Array.isArray(c._children) && !c._tag) {\n            // Append all children from the fragment\n            c._children.forEach((child) => {\n                // For elements, add them to _children\n                if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                } else if (child) {\n                    // Text nodes\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                }\n            });\n            return c;\n        }\n        // Regular element or text node - append directly\n        if (c && c._tag !== \"#text\") {\n            this._children.push(c);\n        } else if (c) {\n            // Text nodes are appended but not tracked in _children for querySelector\n            this._children.push(c);  // Keep for innerHTML rendering\n        }\n        return c;\n    }\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Minimal querySelector support for class selectors (e.g., \".pending-progress\")\n        if (selector.startsWith(\".\")) {\n            const className = selector.slice(1);\n            // Check this element\n            if (this._classes && this._classes.includes(className)) {\n                return this;\n            }\n            // Recursively search all descendants\n            function searchDeep(children) {\n                for (const child of children) {\n                    // Skip text nodes in search\n                    if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n\n                    if (child._classes && child._classes.includes(className)) {\n                        return child;\n                    }\n                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                        if (found) return found;\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching class\n            function searchAllElements(element) {\n                if (element._classes && element._classes.includes(className)) {\n                    return element;\n                }\n                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n                    for (const child of element._children) {\n                        if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                            const found = searchAllElements(child);\n                            if (found) return found;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            return searchAllElements(this);\n            return searchDeep(this._children);\n        }\n        return null;\n    }\n    addEventListener() {}   // handlers are not driven by this harness\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(name + '=\"' + escapeAttr(this._dataset[key]) + '\"');\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        // For text nodes, just return the escaped text content\n        if (this._tag === \"#text\") {\n            return this._textContent || \"\";\n        }\n        // Render element with its children, innerHTML, or textContent\n        let childrenHTML = \"\";\n        if (this._children && this._children.length) {\n            // Render from children (e.g., after appendChild)\n            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent) : \"\";\n                }\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        } else (this._textContent) {\n            // Element has direct textContent (set via textContent setter) but no children/innerHTML\n            // This is the case when _setProgress sets node.textContent = \"X/Y sources in\"\n            childrenHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        }\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${childrenHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n    insertBefore(newNode, referenceNode) {\n        if (!referenceNode) {\n            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Find the index of the reference node in _children\n        const refIndex = this._children.indexOf(referenceNode);\n        if (refIndex === -1) {\n            // Reference node not found, append to end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Insert the new node before the reference node\n        this._children.splice(refIndex, 0, newNode);\n        return newNode;\n    }\n}\n\nglobal.document = {\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Search through all registered elements for a matching selector\n        // Only supports attribute selectors like [data-pending-id=\"...\"]\n        // Fixed regex to support hyphens in attribute names (was [^-]+ which failed on data-pending-id)\n        if (selector.startsWith('[') && selector.endsWith(']')) {\n            const attrMatch = selector.match(/\\[data-([^\\]]+)=\"([^\"]+)\"\\]/);\n            if (attrMatch) {\n                const attrName = attrMatch[1];\n                const attrValue = attrMatch[2];\n                // Convert attrName from kebab-case to camelCase\n                const camelAttrName = attrName.replace(/-([a-z])/g, (g) => g[1].toUpperCase());\n                // Search through all elements\n                for (const id in elementsById) {\n                    const el = elementsById[id];\n                    if (el._dataset && el._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                        return el;\n                    }\n                    // Search recursively through children\n                    function searchDeep(children) {\n                        for (const child of children) {\n                            if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n                            if (child._dataset && child._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                                return child;\n                            }\n                            if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                                const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                                if (found) return found;\n                            }\n                        }\n                        return null;\n                    }\n                    const found = searchDeep(el._children || []);\n                    if (found) return found;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return null;\n    },\n    getElementById(id) {\n        if (!elementsById[id]) elementsById[id] = new El(\"div\");\n        return elementsById[id];\n    },\n    createElement(tag) { return new El(tag); },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        // Return a minimal text node shim — the el() helper appends these\n        // and our outerHTML render serializes them as escaped text.\n        const node = new El(\"#text\");\n        node._textContent = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n        node._innerHTML = escapeText(node._textContent);\n        return node;\n    },\n    createDocumentFragment() {\n        // Return a minimal fragment shim — appendChild tracks children,\n        // and when appended to a real element it spills the children.\n        const frag = {\n            _children: [],\n            childNodes: [],\n            appendChild(child) {\n                this._children.push(child);\n                this.childNodes.push(child);\n                return child;\n            },\n        };\n        return frag;\n    },\n};\n\n// --- mock fetch + EventSource ------------------------------------------------\n\nlet topicsCards = [];\nlet mockSurfaceId = \"surf-mock\";\nconst fetchLog = [];\n\nglobal.fetch = async (url) => {\n    fetchLog.push(url);\n    if (url === \"/openapi.json\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: \"0.0.0\" } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n    }\n    if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/dispatch\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n};\n\nconst eventSources = [];\nlet closeCount = 0;\n\nclass MockEventSource {\n    constructor(url) {\n        this.url = url;\n        this.readyState = 0;       // 0=connecting 1=open 2=closed\n        this.onopen = null;\n        this.onerror = null;\n        this._listeners = {};\n        this._closed = false;\n        eventSources.push(this);\n    }\n    addEventListener(name, cb) {\n        (this._listeners[name] = this._listeners[name] || []).push(cb);\n    }\n    removeEventListener() {}\n    close() { this._closed = true; this.readyState = 2; closeCount++; }\n    // --- driver helpers (used by the harness, not by the app script) ---\n    fireOpen() { this.readyState = 1; if (this.onopen) this.onopen({}); }\n    fireError(e) { if (this.onerror) this.onerror(e || {}); }\n    dispatch(name, dataObj) {\n        const ev = { data: typeof dataObj === \"string\" ? dataObj : JSON.stringify(dataObj || {}) };\n        (this._listeners[name] || []).forEach((cb) => cb(ev));\n    }\n}\nglobal.EventSource = MockEventSource;\n\n// --- globals the inline script touches ---------------------------------------\n\nglobal.window = {\n    location: { search: \"\", pathname: \"/canvas\" },\n    history: { replaceState() {} },\n    // Deliberately NO SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition and NO\n    // navigator.mediaDevices — the inline script's mic block only touches those\n    // inside click handlers, which this harness never fires.\n};\n\n// After canvas.js exports to window, also export those to global scope so\n// bare names like _setProgress work in the inline script (they do in browsers\n// because window is the global scope, but not in Node's vm context).\nfunction syncWindowToGlobal() {\n    if (global.window && typeof global.window === 'object') {\n        for (const key of Object.keys(global.window)) {\n            if (key !== 'location' && key !== 'history') {  // Keep these as objects\n                global[key] = global.window[key];\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n// Node 21+ exposes a read-only `navigator` getter on the global object, so a\n// plain `global.navigator = {}` throws \"which has only a getter\". defineProperty\n// replaces it regardless (Node's getter is configurable). The inline mic block\n// only reads navigator.mediaDevices inside a click handler this harness never\n// fires, but keep a shim so any future top-level navigator read can't crash it.\nObject.defineProperty(global, \"navigator\", { value: {}, configurable: true, writable: true });\n\n// Keep stdout reserved for the single telemetry JSON blob this harness prints at\n// the end. The REAL inline app script uses console.log for debug noise (\"New\n// result:\", \"Server disconnect:\", \"SSE error:\", …); if that reached stdout it\n// would prefix the telemetry and break the Python driver's JSON.parse. Route all\n// console methods to stderr — the same channel the harness's own error path uses.\nglobal.console = Object.assign({}, console, {\n    log: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    error: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    warn: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    info: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    debug: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n});\nglobal.setInterval = () => 0;   // suppress the 30s heartbeat timer\nglobal.setTimeout = () => 0;\n\n// --- load the REAL render module as globals (as <script src=\"/canvas.js\">) ---\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath);\nglobal.createTopicCard = canvas.createTopicCard;\nglobal.escapeHtml = canvas.escapeHtml;\nglobal.formatStaleness = canvas.formatStaleness;\nglobal.getStalenessLevel = canvas.getStalenessLevel;\nglobal.buildContainerChildren = canvas.buildContainerChildren;  // Needed by loadTopics()\nglobal._setProgress = canvas._setProgress;  // Internal helper, exported for tests\n// Pending card functions for bead adc-22b1g\nglobal.createPendingPlaceholderCard = canvas.createPendingPlaceholderCard;\nglobal.createPendingThreadCard = canvas.createPendingThreadCard;\nglobal.splitPlaceholderToThreads = canvas.splitPlaceholderToThreads;\nglobal.tickPendingElapsed = canvas.tickPendingElapsed;\nglobal.applyAgedTreatment = canvas.applyAgedTreatment;\nglobal.setPendingProgress = canvas._setProgress;  // Alias for consistency\nglobal.el = canvas.el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\n\n// --- extract + run the REAL inline app script --------------------------------\n\nfunction readPlan() {\n    if (process.argv[2] !== undefined && process.argv[2] !== \"\") return JSON.parse(process.argv[2]);\n    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\"));\n}\n\nfunction extractInlineScript(html) {\n    // The main inline app script is the FIRST attribute-less <script> block —\n    // the one immediately after <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> that defines\n    // connectSSE()/loadTopics()/init() and wires the EventSource handlers.\n    // index.html has a LATER attribute-less <script> (the agentation feedback\n    // toolbar, an IIFE that touches document.addEventListener etc.), so taking\n    // the last match would run the wrong script. First match wins.\n    const re = /<script>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/g;\n    const m = re.exec(html);\n    if (!m) throw new Error(\"no attribute-less <script> block found in index.html\");\n    return m[1];\n}\n\nconst microtaskDrain = async (n = 5) => {\n    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) await new Promise((r) => setImmediate(r));\n};\n\nasync function run() {\n    const plan = readPlan();\n    topicsCards = Array.isArray(plan.cards) ? plan.cards : [];\n    mockSurfaceId = plan.register_surface_id || \"surf-mock\";\n    if (plan.session_id) global.window.location.search = `?session_id=${plan.session_id}`;\n    if (plan.openapi_version) {\n        const v = plan.openapi_version;\n        global.fetch = async (url) => {\n            fetchLog.push(url);\n            if (url === \"/openapi.json\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: v } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n            if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n            if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n            if (url === \"/dispatch\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n            return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n        };\n    }\n\n    const htmlPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"index.html\");\n    const inline = extractInlineScript(fs.readFileSync(htmlPath, \"utf8\"));\n    // runInThisContext so the inline script's free `document`/`window`/`fetch`/\n    // `EventSource` resolve to the globals installed above — exactly the globals\n    // a browser would provide. The script's top-level init() call schedules the\n    // async registerSurface() → connectSSE() chain.\n    vm.runInThisContext(inline, { filename: \"canvas-inline-script.js\" });\n\n    // Let init() settle: openapi fetch → registerSurface fetch → connectSSE()\n    // creates the (mock) EventSource and wires its handlers.\n    await microtaskDrain(10);\n\n    for (const step of plan.steps || []) {\n        const es = eventSources[eventSources.length - 1];\n        switch (step.action) {\n            case \"wait\":\n                break;\n            case \"open\":\n                if (es) es.fireOpen();\n                break;\n            case \"error\":\n                if (es) es.fireError();\n                break;\n            case \"event\":\n                if (es) es.dispatch(step.name, step.data);\n                break;\n            case \"close\":\n                if (es) es.close();\n                break;\n            case \"reconnect\":\n                // Native EventSource auto-reconnect: same object fires onopen again.\n                if (es) es.fireOpen();\n                break;\n            case \"setCards\":\n                topicsCards = Array.isArray(step.cards) ? step.cards : [];\n                break;\n            default:\n                throw new Error(`unknown step action: ${step.action}`);\n        }\n        await microtaskDrain(5);\n    }\n\n    // --- telemetry ---\n    const statusText = elementsById[\"statusText\"];\n    const statusDot = elementsById[\"statusDot\"];\n    const textHist = statusText ? statusText._textHistory : [];\n    const dotHist = statusDot ? statusDot._classHistory : [];\n    const statuses = textHist.map((text, i) => {\n        const dot = dotHist[i] || \"\";\n        return { status: dot.replace(/^status-dot\\s*/, \"\").trim(), text };\n    });\n\n    const container = elementsById[\"topicsContainer\"];\n    const containerHTML = container ? container.innerHTML : \"\";\n    // card labels live in the rendered <topic-header> text; pull them out of the\n    // HTML the real createTopicCard() produced.\n    const containerCardLabels = (containerHTML.match(/<div class=\"topic-header\">([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/div>/g) || [])\n        .map((h) => {\n            // topic-label is a <div> in canvas.js, not a <span>; match the class\n            // and grab visible text up to the next tag so this is tag-agnostic.\n            const m = h.match(/class=\"topic-label\">\\s*([^<]+)/);\n            return m ? m[1].trim() : \"\";\n        });\n\n    // Fallback: if the label class name shifts, best-effort labels from dataset.\n    const containerCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"topic-card/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Count pending cards in the container (placeholders + threads)\n    const pendingPlaceholderCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"placeholder\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingThreadCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"thread\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"[^\"]*pending-card[^\"]*\"/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Extract pending card details for testing\n    const pendingCards = [];\n    const pendingCardMatches = containerHTML.match(/<div class=\"[^\"]*builtin-card pending-card[^\"]*\"[^>]*>[\\s\\S]*?<\\/div>/g) || [];\n    pendingCardMatches.forEach((cardHTML) => {\n        const pendingIdMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-id=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const pendingKindMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const utteranceMatch = cardHTML.match(/<p class=\"pending-utterance\">([^<]*)<\\/p>/);\n        pendingCards.push({\n            pendingId: pendingIdMatch ? pendingIdMatch[1] : null,\n            pendingKind: pendingKindMatch ? pendingKindMatch[1] : null,\n            utterance: utteranceMatch ? utteranceMatch[1].trim() : null,\n        });\n    });\n\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({\n        initCompleted: eventSources.length > 0,\n        eventSourcesCreated: eventSources.length,\n        currentEventSourceUrl: eventSources.length ? eventSources[eventSources.length - 1].url : null,\n        loadTopicsCalls: fetchLog.filter((u) => u.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && u.endsWith(\"/topics\")).length,\n        statuses,\n        closeCalls: closeCount,\n        containerHTML,\n        containerCardCount,\n        containerCardLabels,\n        // Pending card telemetry for bead adc-22b1g\n        pendingPlaceholderCount,\n        pendingThreadCount,\n        pendingCardCount,\n        pendingCards,\n    }));\n}\n\nrun().catch((err) => {\n    process.stderr.write(`canvas_eventsource_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n    process.exit(1);\n});\n","numLines":613,"startLine":1,"totalLines":613}}}

          </div>\n670\t        </div>\n671\t    </div>\n672\t\n673\t    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n674\t         script so they're available as globals and unit-testable headlessly. -->\n675\t    <script src=\"/canvas.js\"></script>\n676\t    <script>\n677\t        // Session and surface IDs from URL params or generate new\n678\t        const let surfaceId = null;\n683\t        let eventSource = null;\n684\t\n685\t        // Track pending placeholders so we can find/replace them later.\n686\t        // Key: pendingId (utterance_id), Value: generateId() {\n696\t            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n697\t        }\n698\t\n699\t        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel document.getElementById('statusText');\n707\t\n708\t            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n709\t            statusText.textContent = text;\n710\t        }\n711\t\n712\t        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n713\t            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n714\t            const parts = [];\n715\t\n716\t            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n717\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n718\t            }\n719\t            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n720\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n721\t            }\n722\t            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n723\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n724\t            }\n725\t\n726\t            if (parts.length > 0) {\n727\t                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n728\t                el.classList.add('show');\n729\t            } else {\n730\t                el.classList.remove('show');\n731\t            }\n732\t        }\n733\t\n734\t        async function registerSurface() {\n735\t            try {\n736\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n737\t                    method: 'POST',\n738\t                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n739\t                    body: JSON.stringify({\n740\t                        session_id: sessionId,\n741\t                        surface_type: surfaceType\n742\t                    })\n743\t                });\n744\t\n745\t                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n746\t\n747\t                const data = await response.json();\n748\t                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n749\t                return surfaceId;\n750\t            } catch (error) {\n751\t                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n752\t                throw error;\n753\t            }\n754\t        }\n755\t\n756\t        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n757\t        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n758\t        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n759\t        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n760\t        let welcomeProjects = null;\n761\t\n762\t        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n763\t            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n764\t            try {\n765\t                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n766\t                if (resp.ok) {\n767\t                    const data = await resp.json();\n768\t                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n769\t                    return welcomeProjects;\n770\t                }\n771\t            } catch (error) {\n772\t                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n773\t            }\n774\t            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n775\t            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n776\t            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n777\t            welcomeProjects = [];\n778\t            return welcomeProjects;\n779\t        }\n780\t\n781\t        async function loadTopics() {\n782\t            try {\n783\t                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n784\t                if (!response.ok) return;\n785\t\n786\t                const data = await response.json();\n787\t                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n788\t                const cards = data.cards || [];\n789\t\n790\t                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n791\t                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n792\t                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n793\t                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n794\t                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n795\t                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n796\t                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n797\t                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n798\t                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n799\t\n800\t                container.innerHTML = '';\n801\t                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n802\t                    container.appendChild(node);\n803\t                });\n804\t            } catch (error) {\n805\t                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n806\t            }\n807\t        }\n808\t\n809\t        function connectSSE() {\n810\t            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n811\t\n812\t            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n813\t            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n814\t\n815\t            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n816\t                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n817\t                loadTopics();\n818\t            };\n819\t\n820\t            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n821\t                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n822\t                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n823\t            };\n824\t\n825\t            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n826\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n827\t                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n828\t            });\n829\t\n830\t            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n831\t                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n832\t                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n833\t            });\n834\t\n835\t            // Dispatch ack: backup path for intent_ids if the fetch response didn't\n836\t            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n837\t            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n838\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n839\t                const utteranceId = data.utterance_id;\n840\t                const intentIds = data.intent_ids;\n841\t                const utterance = data.utterance;\n842\t\n843\t                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n844\t\n845\t                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to split\n846\t                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n847\t                const createdAt = placeholder\n848\t                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n849\t                    : Date.now();\n850\t\n851\t                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n852\t                const threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds);\n853\t\n854\t                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n855\t\n856\t                if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n857\t                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n858\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n859\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n860\t                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n861\t                } else {\n862\t                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n863\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n864\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n865\t                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n866\t                }\n867\t\n868\t                // No longer track this placeholder\n869\t                delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n870\t            });\n871\t\n872\t            // Thread-level progress event\n873\t            eventSource.addEventListener('thread_progress', (event) => {\n874\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n875\t                const threadId = data.thread_id;\n876\t\n877\t                if (!threadId) {\n878\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: missing thread_id in event data');\n879\t                    return;\n880\t                }\n881\t\n882\t                // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n883\t                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n884\t                if (!threadCard) {\n885\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard not found for thread_id=' + threadId);\n886\t                    return;\n887\t                }\n888\t                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n889\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard does not have pending-card class for thread_id=' + threadId);\n890\t                    return;\n891\t                }\n892\t                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n893\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a function');\n894\t                    return;\n895\t                }\n896\t\n897\t                // Pass progress message (e.g., '3/5 sources in') to _setProgress()\n898\t                const completed = data.completed;\n899\t                const total = data.total;\n900\t                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n901\t            });\n902\t\n903\t            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n904\t            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n905\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n906\t                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n907\t                const completed = data.completed;\n908\t                const total = data.total;\n909\t\n910\t                if (!intentId) return;\n911\t\n912\t                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n913\t                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n914\t                if (!threadCard) {\n915\t                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard is null for intent_id=' + intentId);\n916\t                    return;\n917\t                }\n918\t                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n919\t                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard does not have pending-card class for intent_id=' + intentId);\n920\t                    return;\n921\t                }\n922\t                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n923\t                    console.warn('progress_update: _setProgress is not a function');\n924\t                    return;\n925\t                }\n926\t                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n927\t            });\n928\t\n929\t            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n930\t                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n931\t                console.log('New result:', result);\n932\t                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n933\t            });\n934\t\n935\t            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n936\t                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n937\t                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n938\t                loadTopics();\n939\t            });\n940\t\n941\t            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n942\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n943\t                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n944\t                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n945\t                eventSource.close();\n946\t            });\n947\t        }\n948\t\n949\t        // Elapsed-time ticker for pending cards\n950\t        // Every second, update the elapsed-time footer on all pending cards\n951\t        // and apply aged treatment if they've exceeded the 30s threshold.\n952\t        setInterval(function () {\n953\t            var now = Date.now();\n954\t            var pendingCards = document.querySelectorAll('.pending-card[data-pending-id]');\n955\t            pendingCards.forEach(function (card) {\n956\t                tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n957\t                applyAgedTreatment(card, now);\n958\t            });\n959\t        }, 1000);\n960\t\n961\t        // Heartbeat to keep surface alive\n962\t        setInterval(() => {\n963\t            if (surfaceId) {\n964\t                fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat', {\n965\t                    method: 'POST',\n966\t                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n967\t                    body: JSON.stringify({\n968\t                        surface_id: surfaceId,\n969\t                        session_id: sessionId\n970\t                    })\n971\t                }).catch(console.error);\n972\t            }\n973\t        }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds\n974\t\n975\t        // ── Input bar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n976\t\n977\t        const textarea = document.getElementById('inputTextarea');\n978\t        const btnSend  = document.getElementById('btnSend');\n979\t        const btnMic   = document.getElementById('btnMic');\n980\t\n981\t        // Auto-resize textarea\n982\t        textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {\n983\t            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n984\t            textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n985\t            btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n986\t        });\n987\t\n988\t        // Send on Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)\n989\t        textarea.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n990\t            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {\n991\t                e.preventDefault();\n992\t                if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n993\t            }\n994\t        });\n995\t\n996\t        btnSend.addEventListener('click', () => {\n997\t            if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n998\t        });\n999\t\n1000\t        async function dispatch(utterance) {\n1001\t            if (!utterance) return;\n1002\t            textarea.value = '';\n1003\t            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n1004\t            btnSend.disabled = true;\n1005\t\n1006\t            // Generate a client-side pending ID for the placeholder. We'll swap it\n1007\t            // for the server's utterance_id when the response arrives.\n1008\t            var pendingId = 'pending-' + Date.now() + '-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 8);\n1009\t            var createdAt = Date.now();\n1010\t\n1011\t            // Create the pending placeholder NOW, before the server round-trip,\n1012\t            // so a hung server still leaves a card on canvas to age.\n1013\t            var placeholder = createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId);\n1014\t            var container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n1015\t\n1016\t            // Clear the empty-state/loading spinner if present\n1017\t            if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n1018\t                container.innerHTML = '';\n1019\t            }\n1020\t\n1021\t            // Prepend the placeholder (newest first)\n1022\t            container.insertBefore(placeholder, container.firstChild);\n1023\t\n1024\t            // Track it so we can find/replace it later\n1025\t            pendingPlaceholders[pendingId] = placeholder;\n1026\t\n1027\t            try {\n1028\t                var resp = await fetch('/dispatch', {\n1029\t                    method: 'POST',\n1030\t                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n1031\t                    body: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId, pending_id: pendingId }),\n1032\t                });\n1033\t\n1034\t                if (!resp.ok) {\n1035\t                    var errText = await resp.text();\n1036\t                    console.error('Dispatch error:', errText);\n1037\t                    // Try to parse as JSON for structured error\n1038\t                    var errData;\n1039\t                    try { errData = JSON.parse(errText); } catch (e) {}\n1040\t                    if (errData && errData.error_type) {\n1041\t                        // Replace placeholder with error card\n1042\t                        var errorCard = createErrorCard(errData);\n1043\t                        if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n1044\t                            placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(errorCard, placeholder);\n1045\t                        }\n1046\t                        delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n1047\t                    }\n1048\t                    return;\n1049\t                }\n1050\t\n1051\t                var data = await resp.json();\n1052\t                // Swap to the server's utterance_id for consistency\n1053\t                var utteranceId = data.utterance_id || pendingId;\n1054\t                placeholder.dataset.pendingId = utteranceId;\n1055\t\n1056\t                // Move the placeholder tracking to the new key\n1057\t                delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n1058\t                pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId] = placeholder;\n1059\t\n1060\t                // If intent_ids are present, split into per-thread pending cards\n1061\t                if (data.intent_ids && Array.isArray(data.intent_ids) && data.intent_ids.length > 0) {\n1062\t                    var threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, data.intent_ids);\n1063\t                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n1064\t                    if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n1065\t                        var frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n1066\t                        threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n1067\t                        placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n1068\t                    }\n1069\t                    delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n1070\t                }\n1071\t            } catch (err) {\n1072\t                console.error('Dispatch failed:', err);\n1073\t            }\n1074\t        }\n1075\t\n1076\t        // Web Speech API mic\n1077\t        let recognition = null;\n1078\t\n1079\t        if
