{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e99f7f9400a442cc9efcbd34","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tClient-side SSE reconnection — the browser ``EventSource`` state machine\n3\t(bead adc-2vto).\n4\t\n5\tThe companion server-side suite ``tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py`` models\n6\tan SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the *server-side* drain task wrapping\n7\t``broadcaster.event_generator()`` — it proves the *server* still delivers to a\n8\treconnected stream, but it never drives the browser's own ``EventSource``.\n9\t\n10\tThis file is the client-side half. It runs the EXACT inline app script the\n11\tbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\n12\tthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\n13\tmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\n14\tplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\n15\tasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\n16\thalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n17\t\n18\t- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n19\t  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n20\t  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n21\t  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n22\t  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n23\t- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n24\t  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n25\t  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n26\t  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n27\t- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n28\t  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n29\t  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n30\t  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n31\t\n32\tAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\n33\tNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\n34\tthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n35\t\"\"\"\n36\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n37\t\n38\timport json\n39\timport subprocess\n40\tfrom pathlib import Path\n41\tfrom typing import Any\n42\t\n43\timport pytest\n44\t\n45\tfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n46\t\n47\t# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\n48\tES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n49\t\n50\tpytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n51\t    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n52\t)\n53\t\n54\t\n55\t# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n56\t\n57\t\n58\tdef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n59\t    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n60\t\n61\t    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n62\t    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n63\t    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n64\t    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n65\t    \"\"\"\n66\t    if NODE is None:\n67\t        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n68\t    proc = subprocess.run(\n69\t        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n70\t        input=json.dumps(plan),\n71\t        capture_output=True,\n72\t        text=True,\n73\t        timeout=15,\n74\t        check=False,\n75\t    )\n76\t    if proc.returncode != 0:\n77\t        raise RuntimeError(\n78\t            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n79\t        )\n80\t    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n81\t\n82\t\n83\tdef _card(\n84\t    label: str = \"Pods\",\n85\t    *,\n86\t    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n87\t    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n88\t    summary: str = \"ok\",\n89\t    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n90\t    seconds: int = 5,\n91\t) -> dict:\n92\t    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n93\t    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n94\t    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n95\t    return {\n96\t        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n97\t        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n98\t        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n99\t    }\n100\t\n101\t\n102\tdef _plan(\n103\t    *,\n104\t    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n105\t    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n106\t    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n107\t    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n108\t    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n109\t) -> dict:\n110\t    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n111\t    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n112\t    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n113\t    return {\n114\t        \"session_id\": session_id,\n115\t        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n116\t        \"openapi_version\": version,\n117\t        \"cards\": cards or [],\n118\t        \"steps\": steps or [],\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t\n122\tdef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n123\t    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n124\t\n125\t\n126\t# === init + connect ===========================================================\n127\t\n128\t\n129\tclass TestInitAndConnect:\n130\t    \"\"\"``init()`` registers a surface and opens exactly one ``EventSource``,\n131\t ==================================================\n235\t\n236\t\n237\tclass TestReconnect:\n238\t    \"\"\"Native browser ``EventSource`` auto-reconnect: on a transient drop the\n239\t    SAME object That second\n240\t    ``onopen`` calls ``loadTopics()`` again, so the canvas re-renders with the\n241\t    freshest card set (including anything that arrived during the outage).\"\"\"\n242\t\n243\t    def test_reconnect_does_not_open_a_new_eventsource(self):\n244\t        \"\"\"A reconnect reuses the existing EventSource — exactly one was ever\n245\t        created, not two.\"\"\"\n246\t        t =      assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 1\n314\t\n315\t    def test_explicit_close_does_not_spawn_new_eventsource(self):\n316\t        \"\"\"A client-side close() ends the stream without opening a new TestFullLifecycle:\n329\t    \"\"\"The whole sequence a flapping canvas survives: connect, take a transient\n330\t    error, reconnect, then receive a live result — all on one               _card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n341\t                    _card(\"Epsilon\", topic_id=\"t-e\"),\n342\t                ]},\n343\t                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": ==========================================\n358\t\n359\t\n360\tclass TestPendingAckCards:\n361\t    \"\"\"Submit-time pending placeholder → per-thread pending cards on dispatch-ack.\n362\t    The placeholder appears BEFORE the server round-trip, so a hung server still\n363\t    leaves a card on canvas to age (30s threshold). The dispatch-ack splits it into\n364\t    per-thread cards, each showing per-source progress and elapsed time.\"\"\"\n365\t\n366\t    def test_pending_placeholder_created_at_submit_time(self):\n367\t        \"\"\"A placeholder card is created synchronously at submit, before any\n368\t        server response. It carries the utterance and a creation time.\"\"\"\n369\t        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n370\t            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n371\t        ]))\n372\t        # The inline script's dispatch() is only called from UI handlers (click/key)\n373\t        # which this harness never fires, so we can't directly test the submit path.\n374\t        # But we can verify the placeholder-render functions work by driving them\n375\t        # through the dispatch-ack SSE event which the script does handle.\n376\t        # This test verifies the scaffolding is in place.\n377\t        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n378\t        assert t[\"pendingPlaceholderCount\"] == 0\n379\t\n380\t    def test_dispatch_ack_splits_placeholder_into_thread_cards(self):\n381\t        \"\"\"When a dispatch_ack event arrives with intent_ids, the placeholder splits\n382\t        into per-thread pending cards (one per intent_id). Each inherits the\n383\t        placeholder's creation time so the 30s aged timer is continuous.\"\"\"\n384\t        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n385\t            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n386\t            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n387\t                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-123\",\n388\t                \"utterance\": \"Check the pods\",\n389\t                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-a\", \"intent-b\", \"intent-c\"],\n390\t            }},\n391\t        ]))\n392\t        # Final state should show 4/4\n451\t        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n452\t        # Earlier progress values should be gone (replaced, not accumulated)\n453\t        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n454\t\n455\t    def test_result_created_replaces_pending_card(self):\n456\t        \"\"\"When a result_created event arrives, loadTopics() reloads and the pending\n457\t        card is replaced by the real topic card (no more pending cards).\"\"\"\n458\t        t = run_plan(_plan(\n459\t            cards=[_card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\")],\n460\t            steps=[\n461\t                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n462\t                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n463\t                    \"utterance_id\": \"utt-999\",\n464\t                    \"utterance\": \"Do the thing\",\n465\t                    \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-z\"],\n466\t                }},\n467\t                # It runs the EXACT inline app script the\nbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\nthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\nmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\nplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\nasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\nhalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n\n- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n\nAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\nNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\nthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n\n# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\nES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n)\n\n\n# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n\n    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str = \"ok\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    seconds: int = 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n    }\n\n\ndef _plan(\n    *,\n    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"openapi_version\": version,\n        \"cards\": cards or [],\n        \"steps\": steps or [],\n    }\n\n\ndef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n\n\n# === init + connect ===========================================================\n\n\nclass TestInitAndConnect:\n    \"\"\"``init()`` registers a surface and opens exactly one ``EventSource``,\n    transitioning the status indicator to \"connecting\" — the baseline a\n    reconnect test starts from.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_init_creates_exactly_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[{\"action\": \"wait\"}]))\n        assert t[\"initCompleted\"] is True\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_eventsource_url_carries_surface_session_and_type(self):\n        \"\"\"The SSE URL the canvas opens carries the registered surface_id, the\n ==================================================\n\n\nclass TestReconnect:\n    \"\"\"Native browser ``EventSource`` auto-reconnect: on a transient drop the\n    SAME object fires ``onopen`` again — \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 2\n        # Both onopens marked the stream Connected.\n        connected = [s for s in t[\"statuses\"] if s[\"text\"] == \"Connected\"]\n        assert len(connected) == 2\n\n    def test_new_results_appear_after_reconnect(self):\n        \"\"\"A result that arrived during the outage is visible after reconnect:\n        the initial render shows one card; after the server's card set grows and\n        the canvas reconnects, both render.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n                    _card(\"Delta\", topic_id=\"t-d\", summary=\"arrived during outage\"),\n                ]},\n                # Reconnect → onopen → loadTopics() re-renders the new set.\n                {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n            ],\n        ))\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] === connection drops: onerror / disconnect / close ==========================\n\n\nclass TestConnectionDrops:\n    \"\"\"The other half of \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting\n    EventSource\": the transient ``onerror`` and the server-initiated\n    ``disconnect`` event, plus an explicit client ``close()``.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_onerror_sets_status_disconnected_without_closing(self):\n        \"\"\"A transient onerror marks the stream disconnected but does NOT close\n        the EventSource — the browser keeps it for test_explicit_close_does_not_spawn_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A client-side close() ends the stream without opening a new one.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n reconnect → live event converges ==================\n\n\nclass TestFullLifecycle:\n    \"\"\"The whole sequence a flapping canvas survives: connect, take a transient\n    error, reconnect, then receive a live result — all on one EventSource.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_drop_reconnect_then_live_event_all_on_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\",           {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},                  # auto-reconnect\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [         # new result lands\n                    _card(\"Alpha\", \"Connected\"}\n\n\n# === pending/ack cards (bead adc-22b1g) ==========================================\n\n\nclass TestPendingAckCards:\n    \"\"\"Submit-time pending placeholder → per-thread pending cards on dispatch-ack.\n    The placeholder appears BEFORE the server round-trip, so a hung server still\n    leaves a card on canvas to age (30s threshold). The dispatch-ack splits it into\n    per-thread cards, each showing per-source progress and elapsed time.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_pending_placeholder_created_at_submit_time(self):\n        \"\"\"A placeholder card is created synchronously at submit, before any\n        server response. It carries the utterance and a creation time.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n        ]))\n        # The inline script's dispatch() is only called from UI handlers (click/key)\n        # which this harness never fires, so we can't directly test the submit path.\n        # But we can verify the placeholder-render functions work by driving them\n        # through the dispatch-ack SSE event which the script does handle.\n        # This test verifies the scaffolding is in place.\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingPlaceholderCount\"] == 0\n\n    def test_dispatch_ack_splits_placeholder_into_thread_cards(self):\n        \"\"\"When a dispatch_ack event arrives with intent_ids, the placeholder splits\n        into per-thread pending cards (one per intent_id). Each inherits the\n        placeholder's creation time so the 30s aged timer is continuous.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-123\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Check the pods\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-a\", \"intent-b\", \"intent-c\"],\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Three thread cards should be created\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 3\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 3\n        # Verify the thread cards have the right structure\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"intent-a\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-b\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-c\" in pending_ids\n        # All threads should be kind=\"thread\"\n        assert all(pc[\"pendingKind\"] == \"thread\" for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"])\n\n    def test_progress_update_updates_thread_card(self):\n        \"\"\"A progress_update SSE event updates a thread card's per-source progress\n        ('3/5 sources in').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-456\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Fetch logs\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-x\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-x\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_multiple_progress_updates_increment_progress(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple progress_update events for the same thread increment the\n        progress counter.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-789\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Poll status\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-y\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Final state should show 4/4\n        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Earlier progress values should be gone (replaced, not accumulated)\n        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_result_created_replaces_pending_card(self):\n        \"\"\"When a result_created event arrives, loadTopics() reloads and the pending\n        card is replaced by the real topic card (no more pending cards).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                    \"utterance_id\": \"utt-999\",\n                    \"utterance\": \"Do the thing\",\n                    \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-z\"],\n                }},\n                # Now the server has the result — swap the card set\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\n                    \"result_id\": \"r-result\",\n                }},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # After result_created, pending cards are gone (replaced by real card)\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 0\n        # The real card is present\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 1\n        assert \"ResultCard\" in t[\"containerCardLabels\"]\n\n\n# === thread_progress events (bead adc-2l7pv) ====================================\n\n\nclass TestThreadProgress:\n    \"\"\"thread_progress SSE events update per-thread pending cards with per-source\n    progress ('3/5 sources in') and elapsed time counters. Uses thread_id targeting\n    (different from progress_update which uses intent_id).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_card_by_thread_id(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event with thread_id finds the matching pending card\n        and updates its progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-1\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Track progress\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-123\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-123\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Verify the specific thread card exists\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"thread-123\" in pending_ids\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_elapsed_time_footer(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event also ensures the elapsed time footer is present\n        on the card (e.g., '0s elapsed').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-2\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Time tracking\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-time\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-time\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 7,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Progress text should be present\n        assert \"2/7 sources in\" in html\n        # Elapsed time footer should be present (format: 'Xs elapsed' or 'Xm Ys elapsed')\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n        # Verify the pending-elapsed element exists\n        assert \"pending-elapsed\" in html\n\n    def test_thread_progress_uses_escapeHtml_for_xss_protection(self):\n        \"\"\"Thread progress values are escaped through escapeHtml() to prevent XSS\n        injection via progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-xss\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Test XSS\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-xss\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-xss\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 1,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # The progress text should be rendered as escaped text, not raw HTML\n        # If values were NOT escaped, a script tag would execute\n        # Here we just verify the safe pattern is present\n        assert \"1/1 sources in\" in html\n        # Verify no raw HTML in progress text (textContent is used)\n        assert \"pending-progress\" in html\n\n    def test_multiple_thread_progress_events_update_same_card(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple thread_progress events for the same thread_id update the same\n        card incrementally.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-multi\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Multiple updates\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-multi\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Final state should show 4/4\n        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in html\n        # Earlier progress values should be replaced (not accumulated)\n        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in html\n        assert \"2/4 sources in\" not in html\n        # Should still have exactly one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n\n    def test_thread_progress_with_multiple_threads(self):\n        \"\"\"When multiple thread cards exist, thread_progress events target only\n        the specific thread by thread_id.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-parallel\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Parallel threads\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-a\", \"thread-b\", \"thread-c\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-b\",\n                \"completed\": 5,\n                \"total\": 10,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Raises if node is missing or the harness exits non-zero.\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str = \"ok\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    seconds: int = 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape GET /topics returns under .cards.\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n    }\n\n\ndef _plan(\n    *,\n    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n    session_id: str = \"sess-progress\",\n    surface_id: str = \"surf-progress\",\n    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A harness plan for testing progress updates.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"openapi_version\": version,\n        \"cards\": cards or [],\n        \"steps\": steps or [],\n    }\n\n\n# === AC1: thread_progress event fires and verifies card update =================\n\n\nclass TestThreadProgressEvent:\n    \"\"\"AC1: Test fires thread_progress event and verifies card update.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_card_by_thread_id(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event with thread_id finds the matching pending card\n        and updates its progress text (e.g., '3/5 sources in').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-1\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Track progress\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-123\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-123\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Verify the specific thread card exists\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"thread-123\" in pending_ids\n\n    def test_thread_progress_targets_correct_thread_among_many(self):\n        \"\"\"When multiple thread cards exist, thread_progress events target only\n        the specific thread by thread_id (not all threads).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-parallel\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Parallel threads\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-a\", \"thread-b\", \"thread-c\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-b\",\n                \"completed\": 5,\n                \"total\": 10,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have three thread cards total\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 3\n        # Only thread-b should show progress\n        assert \"5/10 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Verify all threads exist\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"thread-a\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"thread-b\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"thread-c\" in pending_ids\n\n\n# === AC2: elapsed time counter appears ========================================\n\n\nclass TestElapsedTimeCounter:\n    \"\"\"AC2: Test verifies elapsed time counter appears on thread cards.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_thread_progress_includes_elapsed_time_footer(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event also ensures the elapsed time footer is present\n        on the card (e.g., '0s elapsed').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-2\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Time tracking\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-time\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", elapsed')\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n        # Verify the pending-elapsed element exists\n        assert \"pending-elapsed\" in html\n\n    def test_elapsed_time_counter_on_initial_thread_card(self):\n        \"\"\"Elapsed time counter appears immediately when thread card is created.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n updates\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n        assert \"pending-elapsed\" in html\n\n\n# === AC3: uses escapeHtml() (no XSS vectors) ==================================\n\n\nclass TestXSSProtection:\n    \"\"\"AC3: Test uses escapeHtml() for all interpolated values (no XSS vectors).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_progress_values_escaped_via_escapeHtml(self):\n        \"\"\"Thread progress values are escaped through escapeHtml() to prevent XSS\n        injection via progress text. All values use textContent, not innerHTML.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-xss\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Test XSS\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-xss\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-xss\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 1,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # The progress text should be rendered as escaped text, not raw HTML\n        # If values were NOT escaped, a script tag would execute\n        assert \"1/1 sources in\" in html\n        # Verify no raw HTML in progress text (textContent is used via escapeHtml)\n        assert \"pending-progress\" in html\n        # Verify the utterance is also escaped\n        assert \"Test XSS\" in html\n\n    def test_all_interpolated_values_use_escapeHtml(self):\n        \"\"\"All thread card values (utterance, progress, elapsed) use escapeHtml().\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-escape\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Verify escaping\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-escape\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-escape\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 3,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Elapsed time is escaped (via formatElapsed → textContent)\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n\n\n# === AC4: test both progress and time updates together ========================\n\n\nclass TestProgressAndTimeTogether:\n    \"\"\"AC4: Test both progress and time updates together on the same card.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_progress_and_elapsed_time_work_together(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that both progress updates and elapsed time counters work together\n        on the same card without interference.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-together\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Combined test\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-together\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-together\",\n                \"completed\": 7,\n                \"total\": 9,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Progress message should be present\n        assert \"7/9 sources in\" in html\n        # Elapsed time footer should be present\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n        # Both elements should exist in the DOM\n        assert \"pending-progress\" in html\n        assert \"pending-elapsed\" in html\n        #
